SMC++/smc_core_types.pine.reason: Manual publish required; dependency root for smc_utils.

SMC++/smc_draw.pine.reason: Manual publish required; dependency root for smc_profile_engine.

findings.issue: Pine v6 header and library declaration placement are correct.
Export surface is limited to enums only; no obvious illegal export pattern.
Exported type fields are declared as plain string while holding Pine style/xloc/yloc/font/text constants.
findings.context: Examples: string xloc = xloc.bar_index; string style = label.style_label_down; string extend = extend.none

findings.issue: Exported types store varip chart.point fields, which is a known TradingView library compile-risk pattern.
Methods directly mutate chart.point fields; this can be fragile in Pine library context.
findings.context: this.time := bar_time; this.index := bar_idx; this.price := price

findings.issue: Long positional constructor calls for label.new/line.new/box.new increase overload-resolution risk.

recommendedEdits.edit: No change recommended from static review.
First target if compile fails: replace or normalize string-typed style/location/font fields to Pine-compatible parameter domains or a safer primitive conversion pattern.
If TradingView rejects exported varip chart.point fields, remove varip from those exported fields first.
If constructor signature errors appear, rewrite label.new/line.new/box.new calls using named arguments.
If point mutation errors appear, refactor point updates to rebuild points instead of mutating chart.point fields directly.

overallAssessment.SMC++/smc_draw.pine: compile-risky but not proven broken by static review
